rule_set.text_or_description: EC delimits constituencies under Article 113 with constituencies being 'approximately equal' in voter numbers, with rural-weighting exception per the Thirteenth Schedule §2(c).
rule_set.enforcement_mechanism: Recommendations laid before Dewan Rakyat; passed by simple majority since 1973 amendment

rule_set.text_or_description: Original 1957 Constitution required 15% maximum deviation between constituencies. Cap removed by Constitution (Amendment) Act 1962; replaced with soft 'due consideration' language. Never restored.
rule_set.enforcement_mechanism: Statutory; the absence of the cap is itself the rule

rule_set.text_or_description: EC members appointed by YDPA on PM advice. Removal procedure mirrors that for judges (Article 114(3)).

primary_sources.title: Federal Constitution Articles 113-120 and Thirteenth Schedule

notes: Malapportionment is the structurally most consequential feature of Malaysian electoral institutions; ratio of largest to smallest parliamentary seat exceeds 9:1 in the post-2018 delineation. See malaysia-political-intelligence.md §1 for detail. The 1962 removal of the 15% cap is the single most consequential institutional drift in the post-independence EC architecture.
